name: grooming-as-design-session
tags: grooming, design, pluggable, subtasks, decisions, pre-implementation, task-grooming
body:
When a task involves a new architectural pattern (pluggable nodes, protocol boundaries, extension points), run /task-grooming first and treat it as a design session, not just a checklist audit. Create subtasks for unresolved design decisions before touching code.

**Why:** task:686136ce grooming surfaced 3 pluggable-design gaps (protocol contract, registration mechanism, slot policy). Converting them to subtasks forced explicit resolution before implementation — the resulting code was cleaner and the docs wrote themselves from the decisions.

**How to apply:** If grooming flags "decision needed: X", don't note it and move on — create a subtask for it. The implementation subtask should activate only after all design subtasks are done. Three decided questions is worth one clean implementation over three mid-implementation pivots.
